name: Dr Bridget Konter
school: School of Earth and Sustainability Sciences
focus: Geophysics • Crustal deformation • Earthquake and tectonic modeling
bio: Bridget R. Smith-Konter is a Professor in the School of Earth and Sustainability at Northern Arizona University, where her research focuses on geophysics, tectonics, and earthquake mechanics. She specializes in modeling fault systems and understanding the earthquake cycle through advanced computational simulations, with a particular emphasis on the San Andreas Fault System and volcanic regions such as Kīlauea. Dr. Smith-Konter integrates geodetic data—including GPS, GNSS, and InSAR—with numerical modeling to study crustal deformation and assess seismic hazards. Her work also extends to planetary tectonics, applying geophysical principles to understand deformation processes on other planetary bodies. A highly cited researcher in geophysics, her work contributes to improving predictions of earthquake behavior and advancing knowledge of dynamic Earth and planetary systems.
